note: Official expectation model only - live integration NOT executed and NO compatibility/certification is claimed.
authMethod: Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) OAuth2 bearer token. Service principal or delegated user; token acquired from https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token with scope https://analysis.windows.net/powerbi/api/.default. Sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'.

expectedRequestShape.requirement: Entra app registration with Power BI Service permissions; dataset must be a push/streaming dataset for row pushes.

expectedSuccessResponseShape.http: 200 OK (GET) or 200/202 for row push; embed token returns { token, tokenId, expiration }

expectedFailureResponseShape.http: 401 Unauthorized (expired/invalid token)  OR  403 Forbidden (missing scope/capacity)  OR  400 Bad Request (schema mismatch on push)  OR  429 Too Many Requests (throttling)

commonIntegrationFailure: 401 when the Entra token expired or the wrong scope was requested; 400 when pushed rows do not match the dataset table schema; 403 when the service principal lacks workspace access or the workspace is not on a supported capacity.
diagnosticCommand: POST token endpoint and inspect expiry; GET /v1.0/myorg/groups to confirm the SP can see the workspace; compare push payload columns against the dataset table schema.
preventionRule: Cache and refresh the Entra token before expiry; request scope https://analysis.windows.net/powerbi/api/.default; validate push payload against the dataset schema; grant the service principal explicit workspace (group) membership.
